literal_meaning: that which has been put together; putting together
preferred_translation: that which has been put together
definition: That which has been put together, or the activity of putting together; a constructed or fabricated phenomenon.
notes: This entry uses the literal sense as its house preference. The term can point either to what has been put together or to the active process of putting together, so translation should stay close to that constructive sense rather than importing a narrower doctrinal interpretation by default.

context_rules.context: When the emphasis is on the result of construction
context_rules.rendering: that which has been put together
context_rules.notes: Use for the compounded or constructed side of the term.

context_rules.context: When the emphasis is on the active constructive process
context_rules.notes: Use when the process sense is foregrounded.

example_phrases.translation: all that has been put together is impermanent
example_phrases.notes: Here the emphasis is on what is compounded.

example_phrases.translation: with ignorance as condition, putting together
example_phrases.notes: Here the process sense may be foregrounded.
